target.name: Shield AI
target.founder: Brandon Tseng, Ryan Tseng, Andrew Reiter

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $5.6B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 10 same-sector + similar-valuation-tier peers in Defense & Security. Range: $1.4B - $9.2B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

data.narrative:
Shield AI, a Series G defense and security company, is valued at $5.6B post-money following $1.5B+ in total capital raised. The valuation assessment draws on a peer universe of 10 defense and security companies at similar valuation tiers, comprising 8 private firms and 2 public comparables, including peers such as Saronic ($9.2B), Flock Safety ($7.5B), Chaos Industries ($4.5B), Skydio ($2.2B), and True Anomaly ($2.2B).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, applying a standard 1.25x Series G step-up multiplier to the $5.6B last post-money valuation, implies $7.0B. Peer-median analysis across the 10-company cohort yields a median valuation of $1.9B, though the range spans $1.4B to $9.2B, reflecting significant dispersion within the sector. The wide peer range reflects stage heterogeneity and market timing variance across late-stage private defense tech.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.6B (low) to $8.0B (high), with a median of $4.4B. Shield AI's current $5.6B valuation sits above the peer median but within the observed range, consistent with its advanced stage and capital intensity. Key uncertainties include customer concentration, regulatory headwinds in defense procurement, and comparability given the limited number of mature, recent-stage peers in this sector.
